reviews.review_description: In the past 1.5 months I’ve been apart of the discord community 99% of the messages sent are hating on someone. Most board of directors abuse there power to harm, and rules seem to be inconsistent depending on who it is. This game has a lot of potential but it gets ruined by the terrible community and worst admin.

developer_response.body: Its very unfortunate, but there's only so much that can be done with regard to a fan-driven community for a niche platform and sport. I hope you try it again and find some matches with people you enjoy playing with and against

reviews.review_title: Looking to buy? Read this review.
reviews.review_description:
I have been playing this game very consistently since the day I purchased it, 3 and a half years, and over 1,200 days ago to be exact. Here’s my thoughts
Pros:
Sleek game control, feels good to score. Unique ways to develop your style. League makes it 100x more fun to play, most people play this game ONLY for the league. The league keeps this game alive, if this was strictly player based the game would have been a goner years ago.
Con’s:
Puck possession is annoying (While it is not the developers fault since the ping delay and difference / lag cannot be fixed as it is entirely dependent on the player’s internet, it still sucks. Lose Lose situation), almost impossible to become really good now due to skill gap (With consistent effort it would take a newbie months to play in the B league, and probably close to a year to compete well in the A league.) 
Now for some more upsetting cons, the updates are never. The last update was October 24, 2024, the last one before that was December 3rd, 2023. This review is being wrote on August 10, 2025, means that’s 2 updates in the last 616 days. The developer has been open with their experience dealing with arthritis, but i do feel like that is extremely underwhelming for only 2 content changes in nearly 2 years. 
There is no Voice Chat. None. There is an option for four ingame voice lines said by ai, that’s as much communication in the game as you can get. A CVAA law passed around when the game released which made it hard for indie developers to get VC. However i do feel like since it’s been nearly half a decade since release, there should have been a solution by now. I am inexperienced in the law technicality but i would like an explanation on how after this long there is still no VC. 
There is no goaltender mode, you are strictly a player on the ice. The goalie ai is fine, but this feature has been requested as much as VC and I don’t think much wrong has been done to add it (correct if wrong)
The Community of the league has its ups and downs, and it needs to improve. The moderation is lackadaisical and very disappointing and embarrassing. It  used to be very structured but it has completely fallen out of order

So to wrap it up, should you buy this game? I would say yes. This is the best hockey on VR, i think it still does a great job capturing the feeling of hockey, as other games like VR Hockey Reborn are even worse when it comes to possession and lag and delay
Please note though, you should join the discord server and play in the league. Makes it a lot more fun and has kept me coming back all these years.

I can’t find the setting that stops my top hand from sliding down when I put my stick into the ice, I don’t know if there even is a setting that does that but if there isn’t this game isn’t realistic at all because you literally can’t shoot if your top hand is sliding down every time you put pressure on the stick.

developer_response.body: Unfortunately there is no option. Because you aren't controlling a real stick when in VR, the hand must slide down otherwise your stick would go below the ice.

reviews.review_description:
1) Their discord server, they allow you to send people r word threats and whatever but if you are friends with the higher ups you are fine otherwise you are in trouble.

2) The gameplay is very laggy

3) I can barely communicate with my team in game because there is no voice chat which I don’t know why the devs never though of that

developer_response.body:
I apologize for the discord behavior. It is effectively impossible to moderate 5000 members. We do have rules that forbid any racism, sexism, or other unwanted behavior, and a process by which you can report any violations so that I or a moderator can deal with them.

Voice chat was in the game at one point, but the CVAA law passed which made it difficult for indie devs to continue to support by requiring features like TTS

This is one of the best, if not the best vr hockey game. The games are really fun but manual goalie would be really nice. I know stuff like that could take awhile to program but if you get it, it would make it a game I play every day (even though I usually play it every day). I do have an idea for an update. I think adding the microphone feature will help this game a lot.
Great game!

developer_response.body: Thanks, chase!! I'm glad you're enjoying it!

reviews.review_description: The game it's self is not good. I picked right-handed, and it chose left-handed. And there is no button to switch.  The graphics are bad. No one online to play with. Do not waste your money on this game.

developer_response.body: The right handed mode puts the back of the stick in the left hand (like it would be in real life). If you open the menu, there is an option to switch hands.

developer_response.body: There's a lot more to this story and its not worth airing here. I'm sorry you had a bad experience with the game's community and wish you no ill will in the future. That said, you quite clearly broke my Discord rules during that exchange. Others who took part and broke the rules have been reprimanded as well. There is absolutely nothing stopping you from continuing to play the game.

reviews.review_description: This game is very laggy and the puck is so delayed. The stickhandling is annoying because the puck feels like a feather. Hopefully it gets more realistic. It has so much potential though

developer_response.body: Hi! We've just made a major update with 1.35 (and soon 1.36) that significantly enhanced the physics precision with puck handling as well as making defensive play more responsive. I hope you'll try it again soon (and update your review!)

reviews.review_description: This is my new favorite game

developer_response.body: So glad to hear it! Thanks for the review

reviews.review_title: A pretty good start to hockey on Quest
reviews.review_description:
It's a 3 on 3 hockey game with very basic graphics, controls, and player models. While there is nothing wrong with that, it is difficult to enjoy multiplayer because of lag. The puck will rubber band a lot, and sometimes your stick will not register making contact with it. I have tried different servers and played with many different people, and it seems to be consistent in every match. I do have decent internet and am able to play other online games with minimal to no lag.  Also, you can only see one hand on other players which makes it difficult to see exactly what they are doing on their end.

That being said, the game shows great potential and seems like it can have a very high skill ceiling. As others have said, I hope to see offline play against bots. Maybe have team color/player customization (everyone is the same #30 player as of now). Thank you devs and hope to see success and more things to come from your game!

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the review!

Hi! We've just made a major update with 1.35 (and soon 1.36) that significantly enhanced the physics precision with puck handling as well as making defensive play more responsive. I hope you'll try it again soon (and update your review!)

Offline modes with bots and full games is something I'd like to add if we ever qualify for a full store release. Until then though, I'm not able to justify that investment.
